mission.title: crossplane: Support Rendering Default Values from XRD in Crossplane CLI
mission.description: Support Rendering Default Values from XRD in Crossplane CLI. Requested by 24+ users.

steps.title: Check current crossplane deployment
steps.description:
Verify your crossplane version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
helm list -n crossplane 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working crossplane installation.

steps.title: Review crossplane configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### What problem are you facing?

In the current implementation of the Crossplane CLI, specifically the `crossplane beta render` command, there's a limitation in handling "default" values defined in the CompositeResourceDefinition (XRD).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Rendering Default Values from XRD in Crossplane CLI
steps.description:
### Description of your changes

When rendering Crossplane functions, the system currently only displays values explicitly set by users in their Composite Resources (XRs). However, default values defined in the Composite Resource Definition (XRD) schema are not being included in the rendered
```yaml
crossplane beta render xr.yaml composition.yaml functions.yaml --xrd=xrd.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support Rendering Default Values from XRD in Crossplane CLI" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Description of your changes

When rendering Crossplane functions, the system currently only displays values explicitly set by users in their Composite Resources (XRs). However, default values defined in the Composite Resource Definition (XRD) schema are not being included in the rendered output.
codeSnippets: crossplane beta render xr.yaml composition.yaml functions.yaml --xrd=xrd.yaml

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible.
